serving.natural_language_portion: One standard toasted English muffin weighs approximately 57 g, equivalent to a single round split muffin served at breakfast.

nutrient_source.title: Toasted English Muffin — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo database entry for toasted English muffin (cr_id F05030023) used directly. All per-100g macros and micronutrients sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for English muffin (white, plain, toasted), which is the closest analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100 = 70 × 23.1 / 100 ≈ 16.2, using a 57 g serving with 40.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g (23.1 g per serving). Toasting does not materially alter GI for English muffins.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 466 mg per 100 g with limited potassium offset (146 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit toasted English muffin intake to one muffin per sitting, avoid adding high-sodium toppings such as processed cheese or cured meats, and choose low-sodium varieties where available, as the sodium content of 466 mg per 100 g approaches the borderline threshold without sufficient potassium or fibre to offset the pressor effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~70) with 40.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g and only 2.9 g dietary fibre per 100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should treat toasted English muffin as a high-GI refined carbohydrate that can cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation; portion should be limited to half a muffin, paired with protein or healthy fat toppings to blunt glycaemic response, and wholemeal English muffin substituted where possible for lower GI and higher fibre.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (466 mg per 100 g) and refined carbohydrate load with low fat but no omega-3 or cardioprotective nutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease, toasted English muffin is acceptable in small portions but its sodium content of 466 mg per 100 g and refined carbohydrate base contribute to blood pressure and glycaemic burden; it should be consumed infrequently, topped with heart-healthy options such as avocado or smoked salmon, and replaced with wholegrain alternatives to improve the fibre and micronutrient profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate base with high glycaemic load and absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Toasted English muffin is a refined carbohydrate with a high glycaemic load that may promote systemic inflammation relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; individuals with RA should prefer wholemeal or sourdough English muffin and pair with anti-inflammatory toppings such as smoked salmon or avocado to partially offset the pro-inflammatory glycaemic burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Toasted English muffin is a round, flat, individually portioned yeast-leavened bread product made from refined wheat flour, yeast, milk, salt, and a small amount of sugar. It is characterised by its distinctive nooks-and-crannies interior texture created by the fermentation process and its traditional fork-split opening method, which maximises surface area for toasting. When toasted, the exterior develops a crispy, golden-brown crust while the interior remains soft and slightly chewy. English muffins originated in the United Kingdom and became widely popular in North America and globally as a breakfast staple. They are commonly served with butter, jam, eggs, or as the base for eggs Benedict. Nutritionally, toasted English muffin provides 222 kcal per 100 g, with 10.9 g protein, 1.5 g fat, and 40.6 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at 466 mg per 100 g, placing it in the borderline range for hypertension management. Dietary fibre is modest at 2.9 g per 100 g. The glycaemic index is approximately 70 (high), making it a food that can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation, which is clinically significant for individuals with type 2 diabetes or insulin resistance. Calcium is relatively high at 132 mg per 100 g due to milk content. The food is vegetarian-suitable but not vegan due to milk, and contains gluten and dairy allergens. It is best consumed in controlled portions by individuals managing blood glucose, blood pressure, or cardiovascular risk.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a toasted English muffin?
Is English muffin good for diabetics?
Is English muffin high in sodium?
Can I eat English muffin with hypertension?
English muffin vs toast which is healthier?

confidence_reason: Nutrient data sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05030023) at confidence 0.95, which is an approved primary source. The food is an exact match. GI is estimated from the closest analogous University of Sydney GI Database entry for white English muffin toasted, which is the standard analogous-estimate approach per Veritas policy.
notes: cholesterol_mg is set to -1: the HPB Foodo source record for this item reports cholesterol as -1 (data not available); cholesterol data for this specific toasted English muffin preparation is not reported in the HPB database as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this item as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo source record for this food item. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white English muffin (toasted); toasting does not materially alter GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 57 g serving size: GL = 70 × (40.6 × 0.57) / 100 = 70 × 23.1 / 100 ≈ 16.2. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules. The food is of Western (British-American) origin and English is the sole mandatory language.
